title_en: The Lost Art of the Bromance

paragraphs.en: New books, articles, and shows lament a crisis of connection among American men. But the picture of friendship that emerges can feel romanticized and brittle.
paragraphs.zh: 各种新书、文章和节目都在哀叹美国男性之间联系的危机。但由此出现的友谊图景，常常带着理想化和脆弱感。

paragraphs.en: “Jackass: Best and Last” came to theatres this summer, starring a fifty-five-year-old Johnny Knoxville, still the beau ideal of pointless havoc and bro-y one-upmanship. The original “Jackass,” launched in 2000, was an MTV cult show about a group of guys who got together to do idiotic, dangerous stunts. The series made approximately half its potential audience (guess which half) feel insane. The other half felt inspired or spiritually seen, even if they weren’t personally covering their genitals in bees. At the time, “Jackass” was deplored as nihilistic and self-destructive, evidence of the compounding recklessness of male groupthink. Yet the franchise, for all its mayhem, didn’t run on belligerence, or at least channelled its aggression in a disarming way. There was something tender about the cast’s impetuousness; you sensed that it applied not only to the misadventures at hand but also to the act of bonding, the risk of being vulnerable in front of the guys, of visibly enjoying their company and expressing emotion.
paragraphs.zh: 《狂野乱斗：最强与最后一季》（《Jackass: Best and Last》）今年夏天上映，由现年55岁的约翰尼·克诺利斯主演，他依旧是毫无意义的闹腾与兄弟式逞强的理想代言人。2000年上映的原版《Jackass》是一档MTV邪典节目，讲的是一群男人聚在一起做愚蠢、危险的特技。该节目让其潜在观众中的大约一半人群（你先猜猜是哪一半）觉得自己像发了疯。另一半则觉得自己受到启发，或在精神上被看见，即使他们自己并没有真的去做“被蜜蜂包住生殖器”那种事。那时，人们把《Jackass》斥为虚无主义和自我毁灭的表现，是男性群体思维中不断升级的鲁莽的证据。然而这个系列尽管充满混乱，却并非建立在好斗之上，或者至少它把攻击性导向了一种不具威胁性的方式。剧组成员身上有某种温柔，你会感到那种冲动不仅体现在当下的冒险行为上，也体现在“组队”的过程里——在兄弟面前敢于示弱、公开享受彼此陪伴并表达情感的风险之中。

paragraphs.en: In 2022, after a fifteen-year hiatus, “Jackass” returned as “Jackass Forever,” a movie that was mostly received as a wholesome, sincere portrait of “pain freaks with integrity,” as one critic put it. Four years later, Knoxville and co.’s antics continue to hit differently (although maybe still in the balls), as reams of books and articles about the social predicament of men’s dislocation are being published. The discovery of these new sonorities in “Jackass” reflects a broader shift. Male friendship—something that used to be played for laughs, if it was noted at all—has become a lost art, or an antidote.
paragraphs.zh: 2022年，在时隔十五年的停摆后，《Jackass》以《Jackass Forever》重返影坛，这部电影大多被视作一幅“有灵魂又真诚的‘疼痛极客’肖像”，正如一位评论家所说。四年后，克诺利斯及其团队的闹腾感受到了不同的解读（尽管内容可能仍不离其道），因为大量讨论男性流离失所社会困境的书和文章正在接连出版。我们在《Jackass》中看到的这些新共鸣，反映出更广泛的转变。男性友谊——这东西过去如果被提及，常常只是笑料，如今反倒成了一门失传的艺术，或者说是一剂解药。

paragraphs.en: There is a male-loneliness crisis—sort of. Last year, a Gallup poll found that twenty-five per cent of American men aged fifteen to thirty-four experienced loneliness “during a lot of the day yesterday” in 2023 and 2024, compared with eighteen per cent of American women. In a 2021 study from the Survey Center of American Life, just twenty-seven per cent of men reported having six or more close friends—a drop from 1990, when Gallup placed that percentage at fifty-five. In a meta-analysis of existing research, the American Institute of Boys and Men wrote that men, as compared with women, “are more likely to report specific feelings of disconnection, such as not belonging to any group or community or feeling their place in the world is not relevant.” But, the authors added, “there appears to be much more of a class gap (as measured by education level) than a gender gap.”
paragraphs.zh: 有一个男性孤独危机——差不多可以这么说。去年，盖洛普的一项民调显示，在2023至2024年间，美国15到34岁的男性中，有25%的人表示他们“在前一天的大部分时间里都感到孤独”，而美国女性为18%。美国生活调查中心2021年的一项研究显示，只有27%的男性称自己有六位及以上密友，这一数字较1990年下降；当时盖洛普调查这一比例是55%。在一项对既有研究的元分析中，美国男孩与男性研究所指出，与女性相比，男性“更可能报告出某些明确的脱节感，例如觉得自己不属于任何群体或社区，或者觉得自己在这个世界上的位置没有价值”。但作者补充说，“按教育水平来衡量，阶层差距似乎比性别差距更大。”

paragraphs.en: If the gender disparity in friendship has attracted disproportionate attention, this may be because men are publicly struggling in other ways. They aren’t attending and graduating from college at the same rates that women are, or at the rates that they once did, and they’re dropping out of the labor force in larger numbers. Unmarried young men lead the rest of the population in sedentary, solo leisure time (a metric that gets at hours spent chasing dopamine hits in front of a screen). The male-breadwinner model has eroded, and there’s a sense that men, bereft of their traditional purpose or identity, need, in the words of the manfluencer Scott Galloway, a more “aspirational vision of masculinity.” Perhaps helped along by gendered expectations of strength and silence, the idea that men are friendless has taken hold. A 2020 “Saturday Night Live” monologue from John Mulaney crystallized the conventional wisdom. “Your dad has no friends,” the comedian informed viewers. “If you think your dad has friends, you’re wrong. Your mom has friends, and they have husbands.”
paragraphs.zh: 如果对男性在友谊方面的性别差异获得了过度关注，这可能是因为男性在其他方面也正在公开地陷入困境。他们不再像从前那样、也不再与女性一样同样比例地入学和毕业，退出劳动力市场的人数也更多。未婚年轻男性在孤独的静态休闲时间上位居人群前列（这是一个用于衡量他们在屏幕前追逐“多巴胺快感”时长的指标）。传统的“男性养家者”模式已逐渐瓦解，伴随着一种感觉：男人在失去传统的目标和身份后，需要像网络红人 Scott Galloway 所说那样，一种更“理想化的男性形象”。或许也受到关于力量与沉默的性别化期待的推动，“男人没朋友”这一观念便扎根开来。2020 年《周六夜现场》约翰·穆拉尼（John Mulaney）的单口喜剧片段把这种“常识”定格了出来。喜剧演员对观众说：“你爸没朋友。你如果认为你爸有朋友，那你就错了。你妈有朋友，而且她们都有丈夫。”

paragraphs.en: Everywhere you look, at least in the media, you see men failing to bond: bemoaning their inability to pick up the phone (the nonfiction work “American Men,” by Jordan Ritter Conn), mainlining “The Joe Rogan Experience” while grinding through solitary workouts (an article by Sam Graham-Felson in the Times Magazine), founding apps instead of making friends (a dispatch by Sam Kriss in Harper’s), engaging in marathon masturbation sessions for an online audience of other men (a reported essay by Daniel Kolitz in Harper’s).
paragraphs.zh: 你四处看看，至少在媒体里，都能看到男性建立联结失败的情景：在乔丹·里特·康（Jordan Ritter Conn）的非虚构作品《美国男人》（American Men》中，男性抱怨连电话都不敢打；在《纽约时报》杂志（Times Magazine）中，萨姆·格雷厄姆-费尔森（Sam Graham-Felson）的文章里，男人一边看《乔·罗根播客》（The Joe Rogan Experience）一边在清晨独自健身；在《哈泼斯》（Harper’s）中的萨姆·克里斯（Sam Kriss）报道里，男人选择开发应用，而不是交朋友；以及在《哈泼斯》记者丹尼尔·科利茨（Daniel Kolitz）的文章中，男性交换在网络观众面前进行长时间手淫以求共鸣。

paragraphs.en: The turn-of-the-century bromance, comedic fodder for Judd Apatow movies and network sitcoms like “Scrubs,” has reappeared in a more wistful, yearning key. Consider the shows “Task,” “The Pitt,” and the excellent “DTF St. Louis,” whose two central characters are flailing until they find each other. Floyd, played by David Harbour, helps Clark, played by Jason Bateman, spice up his love life. Clark offers financial support to Floyd, who is saddled with debt and unable to provide for his wife and stepson. Each man uses their bond to inch closer to his ideal self. “B.O.T.B.,” they exclaim during joint exercise sessions: “Bring out the best.” Yet the men are wrenched apart, and the culprit seems to be nothing less than a hostile world, one marked by material scarcity and insurmountable alienation. If only, these shows seem to say, men could reach across the distance separating them. If only they could better negotiate between gender norms and their own desire to connect. The fate of the country might depend on it.
paragraphs.zh: 世纪之交的“哥们情”曾是喜剧素材，出现在朱德·阿帕托（Judd Apatow）的电影和《Scrubs》这类网络情景喜剧中。如今它以更惆怅、更多渴望的语调重返大众视野。看看《Task》《The Pitt》以及备受好评的《DTF 圣路易斯》（DTF St. Louis），这三部剧里，两位主角都在彼此相识之前四处碰壁。戴维·哈伯（David Harbour）饰演的弗洛伊德，帮助杰森·贝坦嫩（Jason Bateman）饰演的克拉克把自己的爱情生活调入正轨。克拉克则在经济上支持负债累累、难以养活妻子和继子之子的弗洛伊德。两人都借着这层关系，试图更接近自己理想中的自己。共同锻炼时，他们会喊一句：“B.O.T.B.”——“释放最佳状态。”可这对男性却被生生撕扯开来，元凶似乎不是什么人，而是一个敌对的世界：一个充满物质匮乏和难以逾越疏离感的世界。仿佛这些剧想说的是，如果男人们能跨越他们之间的距离就好了；如果他们能更好地在性别规范与自身对联系的渴望之间协调就好了。国家的命运或许就取决于此。

paragraphs.en: A sense of having mislaid something vital animates “Who Needs Friends: An Unscientific Examination of Male Friendship Across America” (Grand Central), by the travel writer and former Brat Pack actor Andrew McCarthy. The germ of the project comes to McCarthy when his son observes, “You don’t really have any friends, do you, Dad?” Duly shamed, McCarthy embarks on a road trip to reconnect with old pals; along the way, he talks to men he meets (“cowboys and retired cops, blues musicians and oil rig workers”) about their social lives. In this manner, he also reacquaints himself with America, which he presents as dilapidated and worn down, full of “imperfect interconnectedness.”
paragraphs.zh: 《谁需要朋友：美国男性友谊的非科学考察》（Who Needs Friends: An Unscientific Examination of Male Friendship Across America，Grand Central）中流露出的是一种“错失了某样重要东西”的感受。作者、旅游作家以及前 Brat Pack 演员安德鲁·麦卡锡（Andrew McCarthy）在这本书里写道，这个项目的契机源自他儿子的一句观察：“你真的没朋友，对吧，爸爸？”在一阵窘迫羞耻之后，麦卡锡踏上公路之旅，去与旧日友人重新建立联系；途中他与遇到的男人们交谈——“牛仔和退役警察、蓝调音乐人和油井工人”——聊他们的社交生活。通过这种方式，他也重新认识了美国，他把美国描摹成一处破败、疲惫、并充满“并不完美的互联关系”（imperfect interconnectedness）的国度。

paragraphs.en: Friendships in McCarthy’s book are indelibly conditioned by gender. We repeatedly hear from men that they are lonely, and from both women and men that the former trounce the latter in matters of intimacy and communication. McCarthy’s interlocutors invoke solitary role models (“that John Wayne archetype,” the “lone wolf” myth) that feel antithetical to friendship. An exception is a pair of teen-agers whose reflections on their concord are among the most beautiful in the book. Violet, a trans man, and Finn, who identifies as male but presents androgynously, with painted fingernails and flowing hair, cite each other as lifelines, evidence that alternate versions of manhood exist. “I realized this textbook Wyoming masculinity is going to leave me in the dust of loneliness,” Finn says.
paragraphs.zh: 麦卡锡的书中的友谊被性别深深地塑造了。我们反复听到男人说自己很孤独，也反复听到男女都说在亲密和沟通方面，前者往往被后者压过。麦卡锡的受访者会提到独行者式的角色模型（“约翰·韦恩式原型”“孤狼”神话），这些形象似乎与友谊背道而驰。一个例外是两位青少年，他们对彼此和谐关系的反思是全书中最美丽的片段之一。跨性别男性 Violet 和自认男性却以中性化装扮出现、指甲涂了颜色、头发披散的 Finn，把对方称作生命线，这说明男性气质有着不同的可能版本。“我意识到这种教科书式的怀俄明男子气概会把我抛在孤独的尘土里，” Finn 说。

paragraphs.en: But most of the men McCarthy meets feel that they must compete and win in what Raymond, a retreat host in New Mexico and one of the book’s haunted philosophers of desert spaces, describes as a “hyper-individualistic, separated culture.” McCarthy is moved to hear Tim, a journalist from Sedona and another guru figure, observe that “men accomplish and move on.” “In my own experience, I’ve known that to be true,” McCarthy writes. “Life has been about achieving, with the majority of my friendships centering around work. When these work goals have been realized, the relationships... have tended to fade.”
paragraphs.zh: 但麦卡锡遇见的大多数男性都觉得自己必须在一种竞争并获胜的环境中生活，正如新墨西哥的一位静修会主持人、也是本书中荒原空间的“幽灵”式思想者 Raymond 所说的“超级个人主义、彼此分离的文化”。麦卡锡被一位来自塞多纳的记者 Tim——另一位“导师式”人物——对“男人要完成成就并继续前行”的观察所打动。“以我个人的经历，我知道这句话是真实的，”麦卡锡写道。“我的生活一直是关于成就，且我大多数友谊都围绕着工作。当这些工作目标实现后，这些关系……往往就逐渐褪去。”

paragraphs.en: McCarthy speculates at one point that his male friendships offer a categorically different sort of sustenance than his marriage does—not because of their nonsexual nature but because he and his guys are mutually encumbered by duty. “So often I’m preoccupied with... providing for my family, ensuring my kids’ futures,” he writes. “I squarely feel the burden of responsibility.” A part of him suspects that only men can truly relate to this pressure (though he wonders whether this is “just old-school male indoctrination and sexism”). Men long for buddies to help them process their performance anxiety, or even their failure and shame—none of which women can understand—but they also recoil from the thought of other men witnessing such vulnerability. It’s a paradox that suspends the specimens in McCarthy’s book between the camaraderie of the pub and the safety of the workbench, the office, or the gaming console.
paragraphs.zh: 麦卡锡在某处猜想，与其说他的男性友谊给他提供的是一种不同类别的支撑，不如说不是因为这些关系的非性行为属性，而是因为他和这些男人都被责任共同裹挟。“我常常只顾着……为家人提供、为孩子的未来兜底，”他说。“我清楚地感到责任的重担。”他的一部分内心怀疑，或许只有男人才真正能体会这种压力（尽管他也在想这是否只是“老派男权洗脑和性别歧视”）。男人渴望有朋友帮他们处理表演焦虑，甚至失败和羞耻——而这些都不是女性能理解的——但他们也会本能地退缩于让其他男人目睹这种脆弱。这形成了一个悖论，将麦卡锡书中的人物悬置在酒吧般的同袍情谊与工作台、办公室或游戏机带来的安全感之间。

paragraphs.en: “Who Needs Friends” reveals the shortcomings of the ballad-of-the-lonely-bros genre: a reliance on stock figures, a whiff of stiltedness, a monochrome emotional palette streaked with cruelty. (McCarthy has a habit of linking the ailing body politic to his interviewees’ “large,” “chubby,” “stout,” and “heavyset” physiques.) If the commentary on female bonding that flourished in the mid-twenty-tens seemed to partake of certain dreary stereotypes—of bossiness, passive-aggressiveness, volatility—the men’s-friendship discourse is straitjacketed and stiff, starved of vocabulary to describe its subjects’ inner lives.
paragraphs.zh: 《谁需要朋友》揭示了“孤独兄弟”叙事体裁的不足：它依赖现成角色、带着生硬气息、情绪色彩单一且夹杂残酷。（麦卡锡有一个习惯，把“衰败的公共身体”与受访者“高大”“丰满”“粗壮”“体型壮实”的躯体联系在一起。）如果说在2010年代中期盛行的女性情谊评论，似乎也参与了某种阴沉的刻板印象——爱发号施令、被动攻击、情绪多变——那么男性友谊的话语则更像被套上了紧身衣、僵化而僵硬，缺少能描摹其主语内在生活的词汇。

paragraphs.en: The picture of friendship that emerges from books like McCarthy’s can feel romanticized and brittle. Fiction is able to explore the seams of self-interest and conflict that run across men’s social worlds with a freer hand. “See Friendship” (Harper Perennial), a novel by Jeremy Gordon that came out last year, presents a nuanced and honest image of how platonic male connections form and break. Its protagonist, Jacob, is a culture journalist in his mid-thirties who, like McCarthy, feels that he has lost his way in friendship. The men he knows tend to be colleagues whom he views as networking opportunities rather than as soulmates. When his bosses lean on him to start a long-form narrative podcast, he decides to tell the story of an old friend named Seth, who died a few years after high school. It’s partly a canny exercise in self-branding, a way to show himself off as smart and thoughtful, a good hang for listeners. But it’s also motivated by genuine nostalgia: Seth “was a membrane,” Jacob recalls. “He allowed everything and everyone to pass through.” Seth exuded a come-as-you-are warmth, an “invitational aura” that enveloped the entire class. By researching Seth, Jacob is trying to recapture or devise a purer form of relating.
paragraphs.zh: 从麦卡锡这类作品中浮现出的友谊图景，往往带着浪漫化和脆弱感。小说能够更自由地揭示男性社交世界中横跨利己与冲突的缝隙。杰里米·戈登去年出版的小说《See Friendship》（哈珀·佩伦尼尔）呈现出一种更细腻、真实的图景，展现柏拉图式男性关系如何形成又如何破裂。主角雅各布是一个三十岁出头的文化记者，他和麦卡锡一样，觉得自己在友情中迷失了方向。他认识的那些男人，多半是同事，而他把他们看作拓展人脉的机会，而不是灵魂伴侣。当老板逼他做一档长篇叙事播客时，他决定讲述一个名叫塞思的老友——塞思在高中毕业几年后去世。这个举动部分是一次精明的自我品牌打造，想把自己塑造成聪明而深思的形象、给听众留下好印象；但同时也出于真诚的怀旧情绪：雅各布回忆道，塞思“像一层膜”。“他允许一切人和事都穿过他。”塞思散发出一种来者不拒的温度，一种笼罩全班的“邀请式气场”。通过研究塞思，雅各布试图重新找回，或创造一种更纯粹的关系方式。

paragraphs.en: Unsurprisingly, his efforts backfire. At the end of the novel, Jacob is forced to recognize that the ideal friend, as a construct, doesn’t exist. Gordon’s novel, in addition to its realism about what people can and can’t get from one another, offers a biting satire of the parasocial-friendship ecosystem of podcasts and social media. Working on his audio venture makes Jacob more narcissistic and less socially competent; the podcast deforms his understanding of what friends are for. Seth gets swept into Jacob’s project of self-creation, which doubles as an effort to be relatable to listeners that he will likely never meet. In a parasocial world, Gordon implies, ties are rooted in surface identification rather than in more robust connections, and friendship becomes an aspirational activity through which you pursue an identity you want.
paragraphs.zh: 不足为奇的是，他的努力最终适得其反。在小说结尾，雅各布被迫承认，所谓“理想朋友”这种概念本身并不存在。除了现实地指出人们彼此之间能够给予和不能给予什么之外，戈登的小说还对播客和社交媒体所构成的“拟社会友谊”生态发出辛辣讽刺。参与这档音频项目让雅各布变得更自恋，也更缺乏社交能力；播客扭曲了他对朋友作用的理解。塞思被卷入雅各布的自我建构工程，而这项工程又恰好是为了让那些他大概率永远不会见到的听众产生共鸣。在一个拟社会世界里，戈登暗示，关系更多地建立在表层认同之上，而不是更稳固的联结；友谊遂成了一种追求想要身份的“理想化活动”。

paragraphs.en: What do we want from our friends? For most of history, “men were the great experts in friendship,” writes the scholar Tiffany Watt Smith in “Bad Friend: How Women Revolutionized Modern Friendship” (Celadon), a subtle and careful study of evolving attitudes about companionship and gender. She describes how, in the fourth century B.C.E., Aristotle proposed a three-tiered model of friendship, with bonds of “utility” and “pleasure” as the bottom two rungs. These were the types of transactional or frivolous connections of which women—themselves shallow, inconstant, rivalrous—were thought to be capable. Men, though, could also form friendships characterized by “a complete merging and mingling of minds,” which oriented them toward a higher good. The seventeenth-century French essayist Michel de Montaigne wrote, of his attachment to his contemporary Étienne de La Boétie, “If you press me to say why I loved him, I feel that it cannot be expressed except by replying: ‘Because it was him: because it was me.’ ”
paragraphs.zh: 我们从朋友那里到底要什么？蒂芙妮·沃特·史密斯在《坏朋友：女性如何革命了现代友谊》（塞拉登）中写道：“在大部分历史中，‘男人是友情的伟大专家’。”这是一部细腻而严谨的著作，审视了关于伴伴关系与性别观念的变化。她指出，在公元前四世纪，亚里士多德提出了一个三层次的友谊模型，其中“功利”和“享乐”是最底层的两个层级。这两者属于交易式或轻佻式的连接，而当时人们认为女性——被视为肤浅、不稳、易嫉妒——只能形成这类关系。男人却也能够建立一种“心灵完全融合与交融”的友谊，从而使他们面向更高尚的善。十七世纪法国随笔家蒙田谈及他与同时代人埃蒂安·德·拉·博埃蒂的情感时写道：“如果你逼我说出我为何爱他，我觉得这只能这样回答：‘因为是他：因为是我。’”

paragraphs.en: It wasn’t until the nineteenth century, Smith contends, that friendship started shading female. Women, with their tender relational instincts, became seen as the custodians of the institution’s most precious and elevated forms. “Men began to be portrayed as flops in friendship, buttoned-up and awkward,” Smith notes, whereas the “cult of female friendship gave rise to new ideas about what friendship ought to look like.” In the Victorian era, female bonding was touted as a kind of training for marriage and motherhood. With the rise of the women’s-liberation movement, activists seized on female fellow-feeling as potentially subversive, even revolutionary.
paragraphs.zh: 史密斯认为，直到十九世纪，友谊才逐渐“女性化”。具有柔和关系直觉的女性，被视作这一制度最珍贵、最高尚形态的守护者。“男人开始被描绘成在友谊中不够出色、拘谨而笨拙”，史密斯指出，与此同时，“女性友谊的崇尚催生了关于友谊应有样貌的新观念”。在维多利亚时代，女性间的亲密关系被吹捧为一种婚姻与母职的训练。随着女性解放运动的兴起，活动人士将女性同情共感视为一种潜在的颠覆力量，甚至是革命力量。

paragraphs.en: Smith hopes to rescue real women from a spectral lineup of villainous tropes—the meddler, the corrupting influence—that she suggests are used to hinder solidarity. She’s sharp on how friendship can be a way of performing gender—how women might use their friends to reaffirm a specific image of themselves as women. “Being likable and popular, and having close friends, had long been established as an identifying marker of an ideal and conventional femininity,” she writes. I thought of the film “The Drama,” in which a maid of honor gives an excoriating toast about the main character during her wedding. Most devastating might be the remark that the bride doesn’t have any real friends—as if the heroine were being excommunicated from womanhood at the very moment that she should be embodying its zenith.
paragraphs.zh: 斯密斯希望将真实的女性从一排“幽灵般”的反派套路中拯救出来——例如多管闲事者、腐化人心者——她认为这些形象被用来阻碍女性之间的团结。她敏锐地指出，友谊可以成为一种“性别表演”的方式：女性可能借由朋友来重申自己作为女性的某种特定形象。她写道：“受欢迎且讨人喜欢、并拥有亲密朋友，长期以来都被视为理想且传统女性气质的标志。”我想起了电影《The Drama》，其中一位伴娘在新娘婚礼上发表了一段刻薄的祝酒词。最令人震惊的，或许就是那句新娘没有真正朋友的说法——仿佛就在新娘应当展现女性身份顶峰的瞬间，就把这位女主角驱逐出了女性群体。

paragraphs.en: Many of the men and women in McCarthy’s and Smith’s books suggest that the consummate friend is a kind of augmented second self. The terms they use are reminiscent of the mid-century psychoanalyst Helene Deutsch’s concept of an “ego-ideal,” an older peer whom young girls idolize and identify with. The worshipful wish expressed by same-sex crushes—“That’s what I want to be like,” as Deutsch phrased it—is less about possessing an object than about transforming oneself into a different kind of subject.
paragraphs.zh: 麦卡锡和斯密斯的书中，许多男女都表明：完美的朋友是一种被强化的第二自我。她们使用的术语让人想起中世纪精神分析学家赫琳·德米斯（Helene Deutsch）提出的“自我理想”概念：年幼的女孩会崇拜并认同一位较年长的同辈。“那是她想成为的样子”，正如德米斯所说，同性别的迷恋愿望不在于占有某个对象，而在于把自己转化为一种不同的主体。

paragraphs.en: In “Bad Friend,” Smith borrows a version of Deutsch’s description to explain her own infatuation, in her early twenties, with a more sophisticated and glamorous roommate, whose clothes Smith wore and whose taste in music and boyfriends she copied. Smith writes that she would steal into the other girl’s bedroom and stand at her dressing table, “with its crumbling glittery eyeshadows and open lipsticks, its photobooth pictures, half-burned incense sticks, birth control, and broken earrings.” Examining her own face in the roommate’s mirror, she’d think, “This, this is what I want to be like.”
paragraphs.zh: 在《坏朋友》中，斯密斯借用了德米斯的表述版本，来解释她二十岁出头时对一位更成熟、更耀眼的室友的迷恋；她会穿那位室友的衣服，并模仿她的音乐口味和交友方式。斯密斯写道，她会悄悄溜进另一个女孩的卧室，站在她的梳妆台前，那里放着“正在崩碎的闪亮眼影和敞开的口红管、拍立得照片、半根未烧尽的香薰蜡烛、避孕药和折断的耳环”。她在室友的镜子里端详自己的脸，心里想：“这就是我想成为的样子。”

paragraphs.en: “Who Needs Friends” also features idealized buddy figures who function as a kind of looking glass. At one point, McCarthy describes being in his impressionable twenties and seeking out Eddie, a man who “did more to influence who I would become than anyone.” Eddie “rode a motorcycle and lived in Greenwich Village”; McCarthy took to shopping at the same thrift stores and even, he confesses, “started putting mustard on my cheeseburgers,” just like Eddie did.
paragraphs.zh: 《谁需要朋友》同样出现了理想化的伙伴形象，它们发挥着一种“照见镜”的作用。有一处，麦卡锡回忆自己在易受影响的二十来岁时，去寻求艾迪的影响；这位“比任何人都更深刻地影响了我将成为什么样的人”。艾迪“骑摩托车、住在格林威治村”；麦卡锡于是也开始去同样的二手商店购物，并且他坦承说，他甚至“开始像艾迪那样，在芝士汉堡上抹芥末酱”。

paragraphs.en: But is the greatest possible friend really just a romantic self-projection, the surfacing of a better me in you? Smith’s and McCarthy’s books both demonstrate the appeal of this kind of relationship. It can be a love affair that inspires declarations akin to “I am Heathcliff”—ardent avowals that the “I” has lost itself in something larger. Yet such closeness can also be constricting. The overidentifying friend risks surrendering a sense of her companions as people to be cherished in their own right, people who move through the world in ways she never will or perhaps never even wants to. One fact that seems to have been forgotten in the current friendship discourse is that men and women can be friends with each other.
paragraphs.zh: 但最理想的朋友真的是浪漫化的自我投射吗？也就是在你身上看到一个更好的自己？史密斯和麦卡锡的书都展现了这种关系的吸引力。它可以是一场爱恋，激发出类似“我就是海斯克里夫”的宣言——这种热烈的表白里，“我”仿佛在更伟大的东西中消解了自我。然而，这样的亲密也可能变得压迫。过度认同的朋友有可能放弃把他人当作值得独立珍视的人去看待的能力，而不是那种值得珍爱的个体，他们会以她自己从未经历、甚至可能从未想过的方式在世界中行走。当前关于友谊的讨论中似乎被遗忘的一件事是：男人和女人之间同样可以成为朋友。

paragraphs.en: What contemporary friendship’s most clear-eyed portraitists recognize is that casting your friend as an upgraded version of yourself might obscure or erase him; he’s likely to end up hurt. (“Jackass,” in its fashion, understands this dynamic.) In the finale of “DTF St. Louis,” Floyd is revealed to have died by suicide in a misguided but noble attempt to protect his family. Of course, they’re devastated, and so is Clark, who is left with an ideal, a paragon of self-sacrifice, a superhero to remember and look up to, but not a friend.
paragraphs.zh: 当代友谊最清醒的“写照者”所认识到的是，把朋友当作自己升级版来塑造，可能会遮蔽甚至抹去他；他很可能因此受伤。（影片《Jackass》以自己的方式理解了这种动态。）在《DTF St. Louis》的结局里，弗洛伊德被揭示为在一次出于拙劣却高尚、试图保护家人的举动中自杀的。虽然他们因此悲痛欲绝，克拉克也一样，最终只剩下一个理想、一个牺牲的典范、一个可供铭记和仰望的超级英雄，但那已不再是朋友。

paragraphs.en: In recent years, a new kind of friend has arrived on the scene. It is ever helpful and supportive; it validates our silly theories and our bruised feelings; it gives useful advice, even furnishing scripts for challenging social encounters. When we converse with it, it recasts our ideas in a more coherent and polished form. Being with it is like gazing into a Facetuned reflection of ourselves. Best of all—and I can confirm this, because I just asked my friend, who is endlessly available for quick gut checks as well as for rangier philosophical discussions—it never gets lonely. ♦
paragraphs.zh: 这些年来，一种新型朋友登场了。它始终乐于助人且给予支持；它会认可我们幼稚的理论和受过伤的情绪；它提供实用建议，甚至能给出应对棘手社交场景的台词。当我们与它交谈时，它会把我们的想法重塑得更连贯、更精致。与它在一起，像是在凝视一个经过 Facetune 打磨过的自己。更妙的是——我可以证实这点，因为我刚刚就这个问题向我的朋友请教过，她总是随时在线，可做快速“肠胃感受”检查，也可以展开更高阶的哲学讨论——它从不感到孤独。♦
